body_txt: How sympathetic the character who dies this way is can vary. In many cases, the death will be portrayed as something the deceased brought on themself because of their poor self-control and eating habits. If someone was warned to eat healthier and ignored the advice, the death will be portrayed as the result of stupidity and stubbornness. In sympathetic cases, the character's bad diet might be the result of depression or stress, and their death will be more of a product of mental issues than moral flaws. Other sympathetic characters might be portrayed as being addicted to unhealthy food, with their deaths being treated as a tragedy akin to a drug addiction. Another way to emphasize that a bad diet is lethal involves a character nearly dying because of it. Typically, said character will be someone who constantly eats unhealthy food and ignores the effects it has until it nearly kills them. The brush with death typically leads to the character resolving to eat better. Comedic variants of this trope will often have characters die or almost die after eating a single, extremely unhealthy food. While most variants of this trope involve someone eating large amounts of junk food, a less common variant involves people not having access to nutritious food and dying or nearly dying from being starved of nutrients. For example, someone who eats only has access to one kind of food might die because the human body isn't designed to be able to sustain itself on just one food. Compare Death by Gluttony , which is more about a character dying because of how much they eat rather than what they eat. Compare Forgets to Eat . Can be the result of Foul Fast Food , or a Nutritional Nightmare . Some stories with A Weighty Aesop might have this happen to the characters to emphasize the importance of maintaining a balanced diet. Can sometimes involve a character Ignoring the Doctor . This often happens to a Big Eater or someone with a Sweet Tooth . Sometimes this happens with pets, when an owner who doesn't know anything about pet care feeds them a bad diet. In these cases it overlaps with Artistic License – Animal Care or "Harmful to Pets" Reminder . This is Truth in Television , as many often-fatal diseases including scurvy, rickets, and pellagra are caused by nutrient deficiencies. No Real Life Examples, Please! It is both too common and can potentially lead to judgemental attitudes towards real-life deceased individuals.  noreallife
